data.title: Conagra maintains fiscal 2025 guidance amid supply chain challenges and strategic investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Conagra Brands, Inc. (CAG) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Sean Connolly highlighted that Q3 performance aligned with expectations, with strong brand resilience and consumption trends despite supply chain constraints, particularly in frozen meals with chicken and frozen vegetables. Investments are being made to restore inventory and improve service levels.
  • Connolly noted significant share performance improvements, with 60% of the portfolio gaining or maintaining volume share. This was attributed to strategic investments in brand building rather than heavy promotional activity.
  • Frozen single-serve meals saw volume share growth of 0.6% year-over-year, while snacking categories, including seeds and popcorn, experienced a 3.8% increase in volume sales for Q3.
  • CFO David Marberger reported Q3 organic net sales of $2.9 billion, a 5.2% decline year-over-year, and adjusted EPS of $0.51, down $0.18 compared to the prior year. Adjusted operating margin for the quarter was 12.7%, also reflecting challenges from supply constraints and transitory costs.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed fiscal 2025 guidance with expectations of organic net sales at approximately -2%, adjusted operating margin at 14.4%, and adjusted EPS at $2.35.
  • Connolly stated that Q4 shipments are expected to improve as inventory replenishment progresses, though some elevated costs will persist. Targeted pricing adjustments in categories like Hebrew National are planned to mitigate ongoing inflation impacts.
  • Marberger indicated that capacity expansion investments for frozen vegetables and meals with chicken are underway, with modernization completion anticipated by August.

Financial Results

  • Organic net sales declined 5.2%, driven by a 3.1% volume decrease and a 2.1% decline in price/mix. Supply constraints in frozen products were a significant factor.
  • Adjusted gross margin was 24.8%, while adjusted operating margin was 12.7%, reflecting higher costs and unfavorable foreign exchange impacts.
  • Free cash flow conversion remained strong at 125% year-to-date, enabling net debt reduction of approximately $500 million over the past 12 months. Net leverage stood at 3.59x.

Q&A

  • Leah Jordan, Goldman Sachs: Asked about the competitive environment in frozen foods and potential impacts from new capacity additions by competitors. CEO Connolly emphasized the strength of Conagra’s innovation pipeline and market leadership in the frozen category, which positions the company well despite increased competition.
  • Robert Moskow, TD Cowen: Questioned challenges in implementing pricing to offset inflation. Connolly explained that while protein costs have been a significant inflation driver, the company has opted to maintain top-line momentum rather than initiate new pricing cycles, aligning with long-term brand and cash flow objectives.
  • Alexia Howard, Bernstein: Inquired about the Healthy Choice GLP-1 on-track messaging. Connolly discussed how the messaging aligns with consumer trends toward proactive health management, emphasizing the brand's relevance for both active GLP-1 users and those seeking to maintain dietary progress post-usage.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about sustained inflationary pressures and the company’s ability to offset costs without over-reliance on pricing. The tone was cautious yet interested in strategic responses.
  • Management expressed confidence in the resilience of its brands and strategic investments, maintaining an optimistic outlook for recovery and growth despite current challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone from both analysts and management reflected heightened attention to inflation impacts and supply chain mitigation efforts.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Fiscal 2025 guidance remained unchanged, with consistent emphasis on strategic investments in brand building and infrastructure.
  • Analysts maintained focus on supply chain recovery and inflation mitigation, similar to Q2.
  • Management’s tone continued to highlight confidence in brand strength and market position, despite acknowledging operational challenges.
  • Financial results showed a decline in net sales and margins compared to Q2, with ongoing impacts from supply constraints.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management pointed to ongoing inflationary pressures, particularly in protein costs, as a key challenge. Mitigation strategies include targeted pricing actions and productivity programs.
  • Supply chain disruptions in frozen chicken and vegetable categories resulted in elevated costs and constrained shipments, though capacity expansion efforts are underway.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the potential persistence of cost pressures into fiscal 2026 and the broader competitive landscape in frozen foods.

Final Takeaway

Conagra Brands demonstrated resilience in Q3 2025, maintaining fiscal year guidance despite supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures. Strategic investments in brand building and capacity expansion are aimed at sustaining long-term growth and improving service levels. While analysts voiced concerns about inflation impacts and competitive dynamics, management’s confidence in the strength of its portfolio and pricing strategies remains evident.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UBS warns of demand shock from tariffs, $700B hit to U.S. consumers
data.description:
The sweeping new tariff measures announced by the Trump administration could significantly dampen consumer demand and trigger a sharp slowdown in U.S. retail activity, according to UBS research analyst Amit Mehrotra. The effects on U.S. manufacturers will be mixed.

In an April 2 note to investors, Mehrotra estimated that the new tariff regime could amount to a $700 billion tax on U.S. consumers if the costs are fully passed through, equating to roughly 10% of annual retail sales.

“We interpret today’s announcement as very negative for the outlook for demand in its current form,” Mehrotra wrote. “While this is a very small percentage of U.S. household net worth—less than half of 1%...it effectively represents several years’ worth of total U.S. retail sales growth.”

The analysis was based on a bottom-up calculation applying the announced baseline and reciprocal tariffs across key U.S. trading partners, excluding Mexico and Canada under the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement exemption, which covers about 85% of imports from those countries.

Tariff effect could shave 2% off GDP

UBS said the scale of the potential demand destruction could be severe, with the new tariffs potentially knocking as much as 2% off U.S. economic output. However, Mehrotra said there's a potential silver lining if the severity of the move prompts trading partners to dial back retaliatory tariffs, particularly since reciprocal rates appear to be about half the level imposed on imports to the United States.

“It’s possible reciprocal tariffs are dialed back as other countries adjust their own tariffs on U.S. imports,” Mehrotra said. “So, today’s announcement could be as bad as it gets.”

In that context, UBS said any short-term market selloff might become a clearing event, as expectations adjust and potential trade negotiations develop. Still, Mehrotra said the near-term demand shock cannot be understated.

Sector impact: winners, losers

For industrials, the USMCA carve-out is crucial, especially in terms of cost outlooks. Mehrotra singled out Carrier Global (NYSE:CARR) as a relative beneficiary, alleviating investor concerns over cost pass-through exposure. Other firms with more insulated earnings streams, such as Otis Worldwide (NYSE:OTIS) and ESAB (NYSE:ESAB), are also seen as better positioned — thanks to high service exposure and global revenue diversity.

“OTIS is well positioned given 85%+ of its profits come from service, as is ESAB given 80% non-U.S. exposure,” he noted.

Industrial distributors, such as Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) and W.W. Grainger (NYSE:GWW), may even benefit from inflationary pass-through mechanisms, creating near-term EBIT margin tailwinds.

Still, Mehrotra emphasized that demand destruction remains the central risk, and no company is truly immune. Companies with higher consumer or China exposure face the most pressure in the current environment, he said.

For investors looking at longer-term opportunities amid market volatility, Mehrotra flagged several high-quality names with durable earnings power despite near-term risks, including 3M (NYSE:MMM), Johnson Controls (NYSE:JCI), Trane Technologies (NYSE:TT), Eaton (NYSE:ETN) and Parker-Hannifin (NYSE:PH).

“We view [these] as good quality companies with meaningful earnings power over the mid- to long term... to the extent current volatility offers longer-term opportunity,” Mehrotra wrote.

data.title: Akebia Therapeutics gets EU positive recommendation for Xoanacyl
data.description:
Akebia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AKBA) announced Thursday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed Xoanacyl as a treatment for high phosphate levels and iron deficiency in adults with chronic kidney disease.

Averoa, a renal-focused biopharma company, licensed the rights to develop and commercialize Xoanacyl from Akebia in the European Economic Area and certain countries in Europe and the Middle East.

The CHMP  recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization which is expected in approximately two months.

data.title: Canada's composite and services PMI falls in March
data.description:
• Composite PMI in Canada decreased to 42 points in March from 46.80 points in February 2025.

  • Services PMI in Canada decreased to 41.20 points in March from 46.60 points in February 2025.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).
  • Currency: (USD:CAD) (USD:CAD)

data.title: Jonathan Baksht succeeds David Barry as Fortune Brands' CFO
data.description:
• Fortune Brands Innovations on Thursday announced that Jonathan Baksht will succeed David Barry as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective May 5, 2025.
  • Barry will continue in his role as president, security and connected products, the company added.
  • As per Baksht’s offer letter, his compensation will include an annual base salary of $800,000, an annual incentive award target bonus opportunity of 85% of base salary, an annual long-term equity incentive award target value of $2M commencing in 2025, to be comprised of 25% stock options, 25% restricted stock units, and 50% performance share awards, and a sign-on restricted stock unit award with a grant date value of $750,000, which will vest in one-third annual installments.

data.title: Planet Labs inks multi-year deal with onX; stock down 10%
data.description:
• Planet Labs PBC (NYSE:PL) announced a multi-year expansion with onX, an outdoor digital navigation company.
  • Through this deal, onX is gaining robust access to Planet’s near-daily satellite data to inform their suite of outdoor recreation apps, including onX Hunt, onX Offroad, onX Backcountry, and onX Fish.
  • Planet Labs PBC (NYSE:PL) shares down 10% in premarket trading.

data.title: Sarepta falls after clinical hold related to Elevidys trials
data.description:
Shares of Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) fell in the premarket on Thursday following news regarding a clinical hold requested by the EU drug regulator, European Medicines Agency (EMA), on clinical studies involving the company’s gene therapy Elevidys.

Sarepta (NASDAQ:SRPT) shares plunged in mid-March following the death of a patient treated with Elevidys, which the company co-develops with its partner Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) for the genetic disorder Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

In a letter to the patient advocacy group World Duchenne Organization on Monday, Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) said that the EMA has requested a temporary hold on several clinical trials involving Elevidys until an investigation concludes into the cause of the death.

In reaction, the Swiss drugmaker said it is pausing enrollment and dosing of patients in the U.K. for the Roche-sponsored ENVOL study and at EU sites for Study 104, Study 302, and Study 303 related to Elevidys.

“Patient safety and well-being are Roche’s top priority. We will be collaborating closely with EMA and sharing updates as we have them,” the company added.

Sarepta (NASDAQ:SRPT) highlighted that the clinical hold is temporary and noted that it is unlikely to impact the timeline of these studies significantly. “We’ll be responding to EMA in the very near future, and we don’t anticipate any significant impact on the timeline for these studies,” a spokesperson for the company said in an emailed statement.

Update: 9.28 AM EST: Adds comments from Sarepta

data.title: Planet signs multi-year expansion with onX
data.description:
• Planet Labs (NYSE:PL) on Thursday announced a multi-year expansion with onX, a pioneer in outdoor digital navigation.
  • "Through this deal, onX is gaining robust access to Planet’s near-daily satellite data to inform their suite of outdoor recreation apps, including onX Hunt, onX Offroad, onX Backcountry, and onX Fish," the company said.
  • PL -10.05% premarket to $3.22.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Willdan Group secures $30 million contract
data.description:
• Willdan Group (NASDAQ:WLDN) announced on Thursday that it has been awarded a $30 million energy savings performance contract to expand energy and infrastructure modernization services for the City of Fairfield’s critical facilities.
  • The company said these projects will enhance efficiency, sustainability, and operational performance through upgrades including on-site solar photovoltaic energy generation, electric vehicle charging infrastructure, and central plant renovations.
  • Shares of WLDN were down -1.9% to $39.99 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China-exposed tech names fall under most pressure from new tariffs: Wedbush
data.description:
China-exposed tech companies, such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Taiwan Manufacturing Company (NYSE:TSM) and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and others, will be under the most pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs announced on Wednesday, according to Wedbush.

By Thursday morning, these stocks were all down between 6% to 7%. 

"If you start with that assumption then the massive sell-off today (and potentially over the coming days) is a major buying opportunity to own the best tech winners on sale for a policy that will be temporary and not permanent," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a Thursday note. "Especially China exposed names like Apple, Nvidia, Tesla, TSMC, and a host of other tech and supply chain names will be the most under pressure as worries about this China 34% tariff (could be 54% when adding the baseline) and 32% Taiwan tariff are almost hard to look at."

The "Liberation Day" tariffs are intended to be based on roughly half the amount of the tariffs other countries place on U.S. goods. However, Ives points out that the tariff numbers presented in the White House Rose Garden were inaccurate.

"They are a convoluted set of numbers/calculations that appear to be taking each nation's trade surplus by their total imports with the US," Ives said. "To be clear, these are not the actual tariff rates. If a 9th grader in high school presented this tariff chart to a teacher in a basic economics class the teacher would laugh and say sit down and work on the assignment."

Ives used terms such as "Economic Armageddon" and "Category 5 Hurricane" to describe the tariffs.

"We will see an upcoming earnings season for 1Q in which it would not surprise us if many companies/mgmt teams did not give guidance and would take us back to some of those March/June 2020 Covid days of uncertainty," Ives added. "Investors will start to look at normalized earnings into 2026 assuming this self-inflicted tariff black cloud lifts the next 3-6 months."

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
